clean_body: Speaking up for peace and justice in the darkest time of the year. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more | | Send the gift of "It's a Matter of Integrity" by Melissa Chaudhry this holiday season| | | ? Speaking up for peace and justice in the darkest time of the year. | | | Dec 22 | | | | | | | | | | | | As we proceed through the darkest time of the year, and decorate the darkness with brilliant lights, beautiful decorations, and the warmth of family, let me wish you and yours a joyful, meaningful, and generous holiday season. (For my part, snuggling my husband and two kiddos is a beautiful way to spend the dark of winter. So is writing to you.) Let me acknowledge that for those of us who feel keenly the ongoing atrocities in the Middle East - atrocities resulting in the mutilated corpses of children every single day - the effort to find joy in the holidays comes at a moral cost. We are all living through moral and psychological violence - to the extent that we reach out our conscience and compassion to embrace all of humanity, including the most vulnerable people on the planet. "It's a Matter of Integrity" by Melissa Chaudhry is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. Vulnerable people who Jesus (the prophet Isa, in Islam) would have embraced as "the least of these my brethren," and commanded us to love, care for, and stand up to protect. The prophet Muhammad, peace be upon him, tells us the same - "whoever saves one innocent life, it is as if they have saved all of humanity; and whoever takes one innocent life, it is as if they have killed all of humanity." There is no room in any of the religions of grace to justify the murder of innocents. For those of you following my campaign for Congress, I have news! I have formally registered my candidacy for Washington State 's 9th Congressional District in 2026. I will be running again. From now until then, there's a great deal of work to be done. Last time, we had a short runway from a cold start with no infrastructure and still made history - this time, we'll have experience, a great team, better preparation, and far better systems underpinning our efforts. (Please reach out directly if you want to be a part of this! Early preparation yields success. Let's make it happen.) We'll also have two more years - it appears - of genocide unfolding before our eyes; two more years for the reality of the situation to penetrate the American conscience; and two more years for the truth to sink in: that we as a nation have sacrificed our moral standing in the world and most of our wealth - which should be invested in our communities at home - in service to a mistake. There is no good reason we don 't have universal healthcare, free higher education, great childcare and support for families, and stable housing available for everyone. Other countries - even poorer than us - do. We can too. Instead, we have endless wars in pursuit of global hegemony, profiting the billionaires who've bought our politicians, resulting in the deaths of millions of people inflicted with our tax money and in our name. Our Constitutional Republic - experiment in this version of democracy - is not serving "We the People." The responsibility, and opportunity, to make things right belongs to us. Enough is enough. One of the susceptibilities of the human condition is to consider issues close to home to be more important than those further away. As natural a mindset as this is, however, it obscures the reality that we live in a deeply interconnected world that is dominated by one military and economic superpower - our own country. And that our country's decisions and actions have meant life or death for millions upon millions of people worldwide within my lifetime - whether by suffocating sanctions, direct invasion, regime change operations, genocide by proxy, or fatal failure to act. I wish it weren't so. I wish that being a patriotic American in truth - meaning, someone who believes in, and strives to uphold, American principles of equality, liberty, and justice for all \- didn't require consistent, searching, and painful criticism of the current power structure of my own government. But it does. So let us do a little reckoning together. (Indulge me in a particular focus, this time - the genocide in Gaza is the most crystalline microcosm and mirror we have, right now, to see the problems clearly - and hopefully, find solutions.) First: The United States has funded of Israel's agressions in Gaza. Top Israeli officials it would be impossible for them to prosecute this slaughter without our assistance. | | ---|---|--- Amnesty International - arguably the most respected and credible human rights organization in the world - has formally concluded that Israel's actions in Gaza . The International Court of Justice concluded ---- An Israeli historian, Lee Mordechai, has compiled of Israeli war crimes in Gaza - a document stretching to a full 1400 footnotes. While most of us can 't afford the time to read that work in its entirety, I ask you to please download and keep it. And remember it, the next time the BBC (or anyone else) writes a shockingly evasive article, of the type that has within that organization due to a longstanding and consistent pattern of misleading coverage. Even in 2021, American comedian and talk show host John Oliver was Israeli apartheid and the disproporationality of the suffering, and the military capabilities, between the two sides of this conflict. (Feel free to skip the first 90 seconds.) Since October 7, 2023, US soldiers and government officials have undertaken major acts of protest, including but not limited to: A serving US solder and intelligence officer disruptively in a Congressional hearing (one of many, but an exemplary case); Resigning - either or , and in one case, starting a new political organization to on Capitol Hill; And most devastatingly, . And tens of millions of people, the world over - myself included - have taken to the streets to demand peace, justice, and an end to the slaughter. | | ---|---|--- ? Now, Palestinians and Palestinian-Americans are to compel the US to obey our own laws and withhold weapons from Israel, given the consistent and compelling evidence that Israel is deliberately blocking humanitarian aid from entering Gaza, where a besieged and carpet-bombed population is slowly, agonizingly being starved to death. This is important progress, given that Blinken has been found to of Israel's withholding of aid, and has even been (by Israelis themselves) of approving the decision to bomb aid trucks and being a direct participant in their war cabinet. Another crucial step should be to revoke the nonprofit status of "charitable" organizations in the United States that raise funds for Israeli sniper units, who go on to about shooting unarmed civilians in Gaza. And in the midst of all the organizing, we , and . We witness the horrors being done in our name and we . And we learn more about the we were taught to hate. And we remember who , and why. By writing about this, I risk blowback. I risk threats - I've already faced death and rape threats during the course of my campaign. I risk well-funded political attacks. I risk making myself "unelectable." But if I do not write about this, I am not a human being. Let alone a Muslim charged by my creator with striving against oppression and for justice however I can. Let alone a patriotic American who can wholeheartedly claim the principle of "Liberty and Justice for All." To quote the venerable Dr. King, "Our lives begin to end the moment we remain silent about things that matter. " The prophet Jesus, who we honor at this time of the year, allowed himself to be sacrificed for the greater good. For love of all humanity. Whatever your beliefs, may his example inspire us. By speaking the truth with love, no matter what happens to us as a consequence, we become legendary - and maybe, just maybe, we too can change the world. clean_body: We’ll be frank – we were disappointed to see you haven’t renewed your membership for 2025. | | | | | ---|--- We can’t believe it: over 2,900 Democrats have already renewed their 2025 DLCC Membership! That’s the energy state Democrats are counting on as they go toe-to-toe with Republicans in competitive races across the country. But we’ll be frank, we were disappointed to see you haven’t renewed your membership for 2025 yet. Does this look right? | 2025 DLCC MEMBERSHIP SUPPORTER RECORD Supporter: 2025 Member Status: PENDING Suggested Gift: $10 This year showed the nation just how critical Democratic leadership at the state level can be – abortion access, voting rights, gun safety, and so much more are being decided in state legislatures across the country. That’s why we’re reaching out to you today. Becoming an official member of this grassroots movement to elect state Democrats is the best step you can take to help build a better future in the states. If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | | | | | Over 2,900 Democrats have already stepped up and fueled this fight as official members – will you join them today?
